behaviors.description: Reject webhook when `X-Signature-256` does not match raw body; do not forward body.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at handler->queue boundary (must both return 401 and suppress enqueue), not just pure HMAC math. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process HTTP harness is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text only requires response/forwarding logic, not real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with an in-process HTTP test request containing a bad signature and spy/fake queue module; assert `401` and zero enqueue calls.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200 acknowledgment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is ordering/state handoff across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue writer). REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local persistence module (DB/filesystem) without real external network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require real external infrastructure, only write-before-ack ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument queue `write` completion and HTTP response emission in an integrated test; assert write commit/resolve timestamp occurs before `200` is sent.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream event processing to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is decoupling between request path and async processor, a module interaction issue. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS primitive needed; a blocking fake processor inside same process can expose regression. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text targets control-flow decoupling, not real external infra behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP test with downstream processor intentionally delayed; assert response is returned before processor promise resolves.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock even under slow concurrent downstream processing.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit wall-clock SLO under concurrency depends on real runtime scheduling/timing and HTTP serving behavior; in-process mocks/fake timers can hide starvation/backpressure issues. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timing likely passes while real event loop/threadpool contention fails. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly requires wall-clock and concurrent load conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Start real service process, generate concurrent requests while earlier events trigger slow downstream work, and measure end-to-end HTTP latency; assert each response <3s.

behaviors.description: Do not write an event to queue when its event ID was already received.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedup correctness is at state handoff boundary between handler and persistent queue/idempotency store. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is enough; no real network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text is about duplicate-state enforcement, not OS/network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Send same event ID twice through integrated handler+queue store; assert first is persisted and second is rejected/skipped with only one stored record.

behaviors.description: If queue reports full, return 503 and include `Retry-After` header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: translation of queue backpressure signal into HTTP semantics is a module-interface behavior. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; controlled queue-full response from queue module is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text says 'when the queue reports it is full' (interface contract), not that real capacity mechanics must be exercised (covered by behavior 7).
behaviors.test_description: Use integrated handler with queue module configured/stubbed to return `full`; assert HTTP `503` and `Retry-After` header is present and correctly formatted.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow events.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates real queue storage behavior (capacity + persistence), which can fail at module/storage interaction boundary. REAL-INFRA: requires real local persistence backend wiring (DB/filesystem) but not external network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require external OS/network/subprocess effects beyond local persistence integration.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate queue with real local persistence backend and small capacity; enqueue to limit, then enqueue one more and assert explicit rejection plus unchanged persisted count.
